title: Trash/Recycling Service Payment Assistance - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Utility Assistance > Trash/Recycling Service Payment Assistance 23 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness ADVANCED HEALTH FLEXIBLE SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Flexible Services or "flex funds," are non-covered services offered in addition to the covered benefits of the Oregon Health Plan (OHP) through Coordinated Care Organizations (CCOs) to improve care delivery and overall member health and well-being. Eligibility for services is determined by the CCO. Services can include but are not limited to: -Housing support (rent assistance, rental deposit assistance [requires move-in date], utility bills, housing programs for women and children) -Temporary motel stays (For individuals being discharged from the hospital; experiencing an extreme weather event; has a set move-in date for a new home and needs a temporary place to stay before then; or actively fleeing a domestic violence situation.) -Electronics such as cellphones, computers, and other communication devices -Specific educational materials and/or classes -Family and home resources (ex. items for child health and safety, parenting programs, household appliances, climate devices) -Gym memberships and exercise classes/equipment -Health and substance use prevention support All flexible services are determined on a case-by-case basis by the CCO. HRS are NOT intended to address emergency situations. CCOs are not required to offer flexible services. As flexible services are not a covered benefit, a denial cannot be appealed, but an OHP member can submit a complaint. Eligibility: -Must be a current member of the CCO -Member must be unable to receive requested service by any other benefit or means -Item or service requested must help with medical need or life situation -All documentation requested for service must be provided Health-related services are limited, not immediate, not guaranteed or continuously available, and individuals may not qualify. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 883-2947 Visit Website More Details CITY OF HERMISTON UTILITY PAYMENT DISCOUNTS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers reduced monthly utility bills for water, sewage, and garbage for eligible customers. 180 NE 2nd Street Hermiston, OR 97838 Eligibility: -Water and sewer payment discounts: Must meet federal poverty guidelines -Garbage service payment discounts: Age 65 and older and must meet federal poverty guidelines Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 567-5521 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CITY OF PORTLAND CITY OF PORTLAND BUREAU OF PLANNING AND SUSTAINABILITY GARBAGE BILL FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a $21.90 monthly garbage bill discount. Rates are updated annually in July. 1810 SW 5th Avenue Portland, OR 97201 Eligibility: Must meet all of the following: Own and live in a single-family house Be the account holder for both your garbage bill and your sewer, stormwater, and water bill Be enrolled in the Portland Water Bureau's financial assistance program and receive Tier 2 rate relief Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 823-7202 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COLUMBIA PACIFIC CCO FLEXIBLE SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Flexible Services or "flex funds," are non-covered services offered in addition to the covered benefits of the Oregon Health Plan (OHP) through Coordinated Care Organizations (CCOs) to improve care delivery and overall member health and well-being. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Email (503) 416-4100 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY ACTION WASHINGTON COUNTY HILLSBORO MULTI-SERVICE CENTER UTILITY ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides financial help with garbage, water, internet, electric, and heating fuel bills (oil, propane, firewood, and pellets). Payments go directly to the utility company. Applications are processed in the order received, with an estimated wait time of 4–6 weeks. Assistance is based on available funds. 1001 SW Baseline Street Hillsboro, OR 97123 Eligibility: Household is at or below 60% of the Oregon Area Median Income (AMI). See website for a list of eligible utility companies. Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-noon and 1pm-4pm, closed national holidays Email (503) 615-0771 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CONGREGATIONS HELPING PEOPLE UTILITY ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance with past-due electric, trash and recycling, and water bills. Must have a shut-off or disconnect notice. Assistance is limited, dependent on available funding, and available once per household. 1977 Front Street NE Salem, OR 97303 Eligibility: Low-income households Hours: Monday-Thursday 10am-1pm Email (503) 391-4365 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EASTERN OREGON COORDINATED CARE ORGANIZATION FLEXIBLE SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Flexible Services or "flex funds," are non-covered services offered in addition to the covered benefits of the Oregon Health Plan (OHP) through Coordinated Care Organizations (CCOs) to improve care delivery and overall member health and well-being. Eligibility for services is determined by the CCO. Services can include but are not limited to: -Housing support (rent assistance, rental deposit assistance [requires move-in date], utility bills, housing programs for women and children) -Temporary motel stays (for individuals being discharged from the hospital or who have a set move-in date for a new home and need a temporary place to stay before then) -Electronics such as cell phones, computers, and other communication devices when there are no state programs available in the area -Family and home resources (ex. items for child health and safety, parenting programs, household appliances, climate devices) -Gym memberships or home equipment -Beds and mattresses for children under 21 or for adults with a diagnosis of a back or spine condition -Car seats if there are no other county or programs available in the area All flexible services are determined on a case-by-case basis by the CCO. HRS are NOT intended to address emergency situations. CCOs are not required to offer flexible services. As flexible services are not a covered benefit, a denial cannot be appealed, but an OHP member can submit a complaint. Eligibility: -Must be a current member of the CCO -Member must be unable to receive requested service by any other benefit or means -Item or service requested must help with medical need or life situation -All documentation requested for service must be provided Health-related services are limited, not immediate, not guaranteed or continuously available, and individuals may not qualify. Visit website to review additional eligibility criteria regarding specific programs and benefits. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Email (503) 416-4100 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON HOUSING AND COMMUNITY SERVICES HOUSING SUPPORT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Assists with rent and utility assistance (electric, gas, heating fuel, sewer, trash, or water), down payment for a home, or financial counseling, which may include household budgeting, eviction protection, and legal aid. Rent assistance participants must pay 30% of their income towards rent. Assistance is available for 24 months or until the program’s funding is entirely spent. Eligibility: -Household affected by the wildfires and straight-line winds of 2020, which include the 242, Almeda, Archie Creek, Beachie Creek, Echo Mountain, Holiday Farm, Lionshead, Riverside, and South Obenchain fires -At the time of the disasters, the applicant lived in an impacted county -Current household income at or below 120% of Area Medium Income (AMI) -Rent/Utility Assistance: Monthly housing costs exceed 30% of applicant's household’s adjusted gross monthly income or housing is not decent, safe, or sanitary and the cost of housing that meets occupancy standards would exceed 30% of household's income Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 250-0938 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON STATE DIVISION OF MEDICAL ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS FLEXIBLE SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Flexible services or "flex funds," are non-covered services offered in addition to the covered benefits of the Oregon Health Plan (OHP) through Coordinated Care Organizations (CCOs) to improve care delivery and overall member health and well-being. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Email (541) 229-4842 Get Directions Visit Website More Details WE CARE EMERGENCY FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides limited financial assistance for emergency needs such as past-due rent, rental deposits, electricity, gas, garbage, water, and small non-covered medical bills. May also assist with minor car repairs with safety issues and other miscellaneous emergencies. Prospective clients must be able to demonstrate the ability to sustain needs post-assistance. Individuals who have not previously received aid from the agency may be given application priority. 602 SW Madison Avenue Corvallis, OR 97333 Eligibility: -Unrestricted for most assistance -Individuals experiencing homelessness and living in car for vehicle expenses Hours: Phone and email: Monday-Wednesday 10am-4pm Church office: Varies Email (541) 203-0102 Get Directions Visit Website More Details YAMHILL COMMUNITY CARE FLEXIBLE SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Flexible Services or "flex funds," are non-covered services offered in addition to the covered benefits of the Oregon Health Plan (OHP) through Coordinated Care Organizations (CCOs) to improve care delivery and overall member health and well-being. HRS are NOT intended to address emergency situations. CCOs are not required to offer flexible services. As flexible services are not a covered benefit, a denial cannot be appealed, but an OHP member can submit a complaint. Eligibility: -Must be a current member of the CCO -Member must be unable to receive requested service by any other benefit or means -Item or service requested must help with medical need or life situation -All documentation requested for service must be provided Health-related services are limited, not immediate, not guaranteed or continuously available, and individuals may not qualify.
